methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - WDBJ7
signals.signal_reading: AI training focused on worker agency in Southwest Virginia signals potential for distributed deep learning—if workers 'lead technology' rather than passively consume platforms, this counters the concentration dynamic and builds peer-to-peer capability transfer.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: Community-driven EPA workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents distributed capacity-building outside metro hubs, creating infrastructure for peer network learning that could diffuse AI fluency beyond NoVA's gravity well.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's institutional AI partnership concentrates educational resources and innovation capacity in Richmond Metro, deepening capabilities in an established hub but creating questions about knowledge transfer mechanisms to rural regions.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Platform-delivered AI personalization tools in Hampton Roads real estate demonstrate shallow, broad adoption—users gain access without building underlying capability, signaling distribution without depth across the region.

signals.title: UVA researchers use AI to speed up drug development - WVIR
signals.signal_reading: UVA's AI-accelerated drug development concentrates advanced research capability in academic institutions, reinforcing the metro-university excellence pattern while the application remains specialized rather than distributed to broader workforce development.
